name: Rack of Lamb with Herb Sauce
recipeInstructions:
------------
Lamb:
1. Dice the carrots, celery, and onions.
2. Coarsely chop the thyme, bay leaves, rosemary, parsley, and garlic.
3. Mix all ingredients with oil in a large bowl to make marinade.
4. Rub mixture over lamb racks (4 oz ham cooked, julienned).
5. Marinate for 48 hours, turning occasionally.
6. Remove lamb from marinade and drain.
7. Strain vegetables from marinade and reserve.
8. In a roasting pan, brown bones at 500°F for 15 minutes.
9. Add strained vegetables, then brown another 5 minutes.
10. Strain contents, add demi-glace (lamb or veal), bouquet garni, and cook for 2 hours,
skimming carefully and adjusting seasoning.
11. Strain sauce through chinois into a pan, heat.
12. Add julienned ham, pickle, and mushroom tops; adjust seasoning.
13. Season lamb racks with thyme, place in roasting pan, and roast at 375°F for 15-18 minutes.
14. Let rest for 5 minutes before carving.
15. Blanch carrots (reserve) and snow peas (season with salt, sugar, black pepper); sauté in butter.
16. Cut lamb into halves, each yielding four ribs per person.
17. Warm sauce and serve alongside racks.
18. Garnish with sautéed carrots, snow peas, and watercress.
